Class TopArtistsViewModel

java.lang.Object
com.sebastiandorata.musicdashboard.presentation.Analytics.viewmodel.TopArtistsViewModel
@Service public class TopArtistsViewModel extends Object
ViewModel for artist-related analytics views.

Serves two distinct consumers with separate methods:

  • The dashboard top-5 panel via loadTopArtistsData(Consumer, int), which returns only artists with valid play history, ranked by listening time and padded to exactly 5 rows.
  • The analytics "View All" modal via buildAllArtistsRankedData(), which returns every artist in the library ranked by listening time, including unplayed artists with a time of zero.

Both methods perform a single database fetch of playback history and derive all required values in memory, avoiding N+1 query patterns.

    • TopArtistsViewModel

      public TopArtistsViewModel()

  • Method Details

    • loadTopArtistsData

      public void loadTopArtistsData(Consumer<List<TopArtistRowData>> onSuccess, int limit)
      Loads a ranked list of top artists asynchronously for display in a preview or panel, delivering results to onSuccess on the JavaFX thread.

      Delegates to buildTopArtistsData(int) on a background thread via DataLoadingService. The limit parameter controls how many rows are returned, allowing the dashboard panel and analytics preview to share this method with different display sizes.

      Parameters:
      onSuccess - callback invoked on the JavaFX thread with the completed list of exactly limit TopArtistRowData rows
      limit - the number of rows to return; rows are padded with placeholders if fewer than limit artists have valid play history

    • buildTopArtistsData

      public List<TopArtistRowData> buildTopArtistsData(int limit)
      Builds and returns a ranked list of top artist rows synchronously, ranked by total listening time descending, bounded to limit rows.

      Optimization: Replaces the original implementation that made one ArtistListeningTimeService.getArtistTotalSeconds(Artist) call per artist, each triggering a full table scan — resulting in up to N+1 sequential database round trips per refresh. This method performs a single database fetch and accumulates total seconds per artist in memory using a HashMap in one pass before sorting and slicing to limit. The formatted time string is derived from the in-memory total via ArtistListeningTimeService.ArtistTimeData.formatTime(), requiring no additional queries.

      If fewer than limit artists exist in the playback history, the result is padded with placeholder entries (null artist, "—" for name and time) so the calling panel always renders a full list of the expected size.

      Time Complexity: O(n) where n = total valid playback history rows. One pass to accumulate artist totals, one pass to sort bounded by the number of distinct artists, and one pass to map to DTOs. Space Complexity: O(a) where a = number of distinct artists in playback history. The result list is always O(limit).

      Parameters:
      limit - the maximum number of rows to return
      Returns:
      a list of exactly limit TopArtistRowData entries, padded with placeholders if fewer than limit artists have valid play history

    • buildAllArtistsRankedData

      public DoublyLinkedList<TopArtistRowData> buildAllArtistsRankedData()

      Builds all artist rows for the analytics modal synchronously, ranked by total listening time descending. All artists in the library are included.

      Those with no valid plays display a formatted time of "0m" and an empty name field in the modal row.

      Used exclusively by the analytics "View All" modal path via GenericModalLoader. The dashboard top-5 panel uses buildTopArtistsData(int) instead, keeping that path fast and unaffected by library size.

      Performs two database fetches. One fetch for playback history and one for the full artist list, then joins them in memory. The result is cached by AnalyticsCacheService so subsequent modal opens within the same session incur no additional database cost.

      Time Complexity: O(n + a) where n = valid playback history rows and a = total artists in the library. One pass to build the seconds map, one pass to sort all artists, one pass to map to DTOs. Space Complexity: O(a) for the full ranked result list.

      Returns:
      a DoublyLinkedList of TopArtistRowData for every artist in the library, with zero-time entries at the bottom for artists that have no valid play history
